raw.title: A realistic ‘energy transition’ is to get better at using less of it
raw.excerpt: We must develop a realistic plan for energy descent, rather than clinging to naive fantasies of endless consumer abundance powered by alternatives to fossil fuels.

llm_processed.summary: A realistic energy transition is about reducing energy use, not just switching to cleaner sources. This means changing our behavior and using technology to improve energy efficiency.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: This idea could enable a deeper conversation about the role of consumption in our energy transition, and how to design a society that values both personal happiness and planetary health.
